L1.area: Cryptographic message construction and signing operations

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the message signing functionality where a temporary memory buffer object wrapping caller-provided data is created and immediately dropped while a raw pointer to its internal state is retained. The dangling pointer is subsequently passed to a foreign function interface call, resulting in access to freed memory that could lead to crashes or arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: CMS (Cryptographic Message Syntax) content signing and encoding
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the CMS message signing functionality where a temporary memory buffer object wrapping caller-provided data is created inline and immediately dropped when the enclosing expression completes. A raw pointer extracted from the temporary is retained and later passed to the underlying C library's signing function, resulting in access to freed memory that could lead to crashes or arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: In the CMS signing function, a MemBioSlice temporary is created and its raw pointer extracted in a single expression, causing the MemBioSlice to be dropped immediately while the dangling pointer is passed to the C library's CMS_sign function.
